patch.value: Dogfood 2026-05-21 follow-ups: test --add key validation, semantic-fallback labeling, close active-children info, stale blocker on close

Manual dogfood findings not yet fixed (separate from pm-5k2w which holds the 2026-05-20 polish backlog). B2 [med]: pm test --add 'cmd=...,name=...' silently stores the whole pair as the command - reject/warn unknown keys, accept cmd as alias for command. S1 [med]: when no embedding provider is configured but ollama auto-defaults engage, search reports mode:semantic with no warning even though embedded_items=0 - clarify degraded/lexical state. C3 [med]: closing a parent/epic with active children under minimal governance is silent - emit an informational closed_with_active_children warning even when not blocking. C4 [low]: closed items retain blocked_by metadata - clear/annotate on terminal transition. U2 [low]: did-you-mean missing for unknown options (--statuss). U3 [low]: accept --list as alias for --explore on package/extension. CAL1 [med]: support compound relative event times (start=+1d,end=+1d+2h) or a duration= field.
Each listed finding is fixed or explicitly deferred with rationale; no agent-blocking introduced.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Coordinating pm-fu5d findings: C3/C4/U2/U3 by me, S1/B2 by query-ux sub-agent (comments on this item).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: C3 + C4 FIXED (pm-uz25 step 2) in src/cli/commands/close.ts. C3: active-children are now scanned even under minimal governance; off-mode close emits an informational closed_with_active_children:<id>:<children> warning (never blocks), distinct from the warn/strict close_validation_active_children. C4: terminal close now clears the stale active blocker — scalar blocked_by + blocked_reason and any blocked_by dependency edges (preserving the pm-kyd6 scalar/graph invariant) — and annotates with closed_cleared_blocked_by:<id>:<previousBlocker>. 2 regression tests added; close-command.spec 11/11 green. Remaining fu5d items: U2/U3 (me, next), S1/B2 (query-ux sub-agent), CAL1 (calendar-ux sub-agent).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: U2 + U3 FIXED (pm-uz25 step 3). U2 (did-you-mean for --statuss): the unknown-option suggestion + never-block typo-normalization infra already existed, but LIST_FILTER_FLAG_CONTRACTS was missing --status (the only list flag absent vs `pm list --help`). Added { flag: '--status' } so `pm list --statuss open` now auto-corrects to --status (flag_typo, high confidence) exactly like every other list flag. U3: added `--list` as an alias for `--explore` on package/extension (register-setup.ts: option + readBoolean('explore','list'); cli-contracts EXTENSION_FLAG_CONTRACTS += --list). Verified: `pm package --list` / `pm extension --list` => action:explore. Tests: bootstrap-args.spec U2 case + cli.integration.spec U3 case, both green.

value.author: query-ux-subagent
value.text: query-ux-subagent START: implementing S1 (search degraded labeling) + B2 (test --add cmd alias + unknown-key validation).

value.author: query-ux-subagent
value.text:
query-ux-subagent DONE: S1 + B2 implemented, type-check clean, focused specs 73/73 green.

S1 (search degraded/lexical labeling) — src/cli/commands/search.ts:
- computeSemanticOrHybridHits now returns { hits, embeddedItems } (was SearchHit[]). embeddedItems = semanticScores.size (count of docs that actually matched the vector/embedding corpus). New SemanticQueryResult interface added.
- In runSearch main flow, after a SUCCESSFUL built-in semantic/hybrid run (no fallback thrown), if embeddedItems === 0 we push warning: search_${effectiveMode}_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical -> i.e. 'search_semantic_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical' and 'search_hybrid_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical'. Reported mode is intentionally UNCHANGED (stays semantic/hybrid) per spec; only the warning makes the degraded/lexical state observable. Ranking/threshold/limit/projection untouched. Never errors (additive warning only). Distinct from the pre-existing search_<mode>_fallback:* warnings (which fire when the backend throws/degrades to keyword).
- Note: the empty-CORPUS path (items exist, semantic ran, 0 vector matches) is separate from the empty-RESULTSET early-return (filteredDocuments.length===0) which still legitimately reports mode without a warning.

B2 (test --add cmd alias + unknown-key validation) — src/cli/commands/test.ts:
- looksLikeStructuredLinkedTestEntry regex now includes 'cmd'.
- Added STRUCTURED_LINKED_TEST_ADD_KEYS allowlist (command, cmd, path, scope, timeout, timeout_seconds, pm_context_mode, env_set, env_clear, shared_host_safe, assert_stdout_contains, assert_stdout_regex, assert_stderr_contains, assert_stderr_regex, assert_stdout_min_lines, assert_json_field_equals, assert_json_field_gte, note) + normalizeStructuredAddEntry().
- parseAddEntries now runs structured kv through normalizeStructuredAddEntry: throws PmCliError(USAGE) on any unknown key — message: '--add does not recognize key(s) "<key>". Allowed keys: command, cmd, path, ...'. Folds cmd->command; if both command and cmd given and differ, throws '--add command and cmd must match when both are provided'. Bare commands (incl. those containing = like '--reporter=dot') still pass through unchanged.
- Fixes the original bug where 'cmd=...,name=...' was silently stored verbatim as the command.

Tests added:
- tests/unit/test-command.spec.ts: 3 new it() blocks — cmd alias maps to command; unknown key (name/bogus) and command/cmd conflict rejected (USAGE) with nothing stored; bare command containing = preserved.
- tests/unit/search-command.spec.ts: 2 new it() blocks — empty embedding corpus (ext vector adapter returns []) emits the degraded warning for both semantic and hybrid; non-empty corpus does NOT emit it.

Verification: npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json => clean (exit 0). PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1 run-tests.mjs test (both specs) => Test Files 2 passed, Tests 73 passed.

Shared/forbidden files I did NOT touch but recommend the coordinator update:
1. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry (suggested text below).
2. src/cli/commands/completion.ts and/or contracts: if --add key completions are enumerated anywhere, add 'cmd'. (I did not verify whether they enumerate these keys — coordinator should grep for the existing key list e.g. 'shared_host_safe' in completion/contracts/docs.)
3. docs: any 'pm test --add' reference doc should mention the cmd alias and that unknown keys now error.

Suggested CHANGELOG [Unreleased] lines:
- Added: 'pm search --mode semantic|hybrid' now emits a 'search_<mode>_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical' warning when the embedding corpus is empty, so agents can tell results are effectively lexical even though mode stays semantic/hybrid.
- Added: 'pm test --add' accepts 'cmd' as an alias for 'command'.
- Fixed: 'pm test --add' now errors on unknown keys (e.g. name=) instead of silently storing the whole 'cmd=...,name=...' string as the command.

value.author: calendar-ux-subagent
value.text: calendar-ux-subagent: CAL1 (duration= on --event) is done as part of pm-uzmf. duration=<relative> (e.g. duration=2h, duration=+1d) now derives event end from startAt in both parse sites (pm create parseEvents and pm update parseEventEntries). end + duration are mutually exclusive. Units are h/d/w/m where m=MONTHS (no minutes token). See pm-uzmf comment for full details and test coverage.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: VERIFICATION 2026-05-23 (coordinator): all sub-items confirmed present in main @398fb079 (PR #45) + linked-test parsing @PR#46. Dogfooded in temp dir against freshly-built dist: B2 — `pm test --add 'cmd=echo hi,name=bogus'` rejects with `--add does not recognize key "name". Allowed keys: command, cmd, path, ...` (USAGE); `cmd=echo ok` folds to command:'echo ok'. cmd alias + unknown-key validation live in src/cli/commands/linked-test-entry.ts. S1 — search.ts:1338 pushes `search_<mode>_degraded:no_vector_matches:results_are_lexical` after a successful semantic/hybrid run with an empty vector corpus (warning code differs from the earlier comment's `no_embedded_items` wording but behavior is equivalent). C3/C4 — close.ts:158/200 emit closed_with_active_children + closed_cleared_blocked_by. U2/U3 — --status typo normalization + --list alias confirmed in prior commit. CAL1 — duration= confirmed (see below). Nothing outstanding.

patch.value: All sub-items (B2/S1/C3/C4/U2/U3/CAL1) implemented and merged to main (PR #45 commit 398fb079; linked-test parsing PR #46) and re-verified by dogfooding freshly-built dist in a temp dir on 2026-05-23. No outstanding work.

author: codex-active-work

patch.value: Implemented and merged all dogfood follow-ups: linked-test add key validation, semantic fallback labeling, active-child close warning, stale blocker clearing, option typo normalization, package list alias, and calendar duration ergonomics.
Each listed dogfood finding is fixed or explicitly documented with no outstanding agent-blocking behavior.
All sub-items were verified in main and the item was closed with dogfood evidence on 2026-05-23.
